content.text:
Done! Here's what I added:

**Home page** (`/`):
- New "mini apps" section with your icon, a brief description, and "View all" link pointing to `/mini-apps`

**New mini-apps page** (`/mini-apps`):
- Clean page with back navigation to home
- Shows the icon and header
- List of mini apps with GitHub/live demo links
- Currently has `clipboard` as a starter entry

To add more mini apps later, just add entries to the `miniApps` array in `app/mini-apps/page.tsx`:

```ts
{
    title: "app-name",
    description: "what it does",
    github: "https://github.com/...",
    url: "https://optional-live-demo.com",  // optional
}
```
Got it! Let me update the mini-apps page to match that design with icons, tags, dates, and the ↗ links style.

mac?: string } const miniApps: MiniApp[] = [ { title: "opennote", description: "turn claude into a note taking app", tags: ["productivity"], date: "mar 2026", icon: "📝", live: "#", code: "#", }, { ["productivity"], date: "dec 2025", icon: "✏️", live: "#", code: "#", }, { title: "obsidian auto title", description: "obsidian plugin that automatically generates titles for your notes so you can keep writing without pausing to think of one.", tags: ["obsidian", "plugin"], date: "dec 2025", icon: "💎", code: "#", }, { title: "marvel moonknight portfolio", description: "a portfolio website code: "#", }, { title: "agentic patterns playbook", description: "interactive reference for ai agentic design patterns. a quick playbook to understand how agents are built.", tags: ["AI", content.text:
Done! I've updated both pages to match the design:

**Home page section:**
- Monospace font with bold "mini apps" title
- "NEW" badge
- Clickable title/icon that links to mini-apps page
- Monospace description text

**Mini apps page:**
- Clean layout with emoji icons on the left
- App name in bold monospace, followed by tags (bordered pills) and date
- Description below
- Right-aligned action links (↗ live, ↗ code, ↗ mac, ↗ demo)
- Divider lines between items
- "NEW" badge next to title

To add your real apps, update the `miniApps` array in `app/mini-apps/page.tsx` - each entry has:
```ts
{
    title: "app-name",
    description: "what it does",
    tags: ["category"],
    date: "mon year",
    icon: "🎨",  // emoji
    live: "https://...",  // optional
    code: "https://...",  // optional
    mac: "https://...",   // optional
    demo: "https://...",  // optional
}
```

content.text:
Done. I've simplified both pages to match your site's existing aesthetic:

**Home page:**
- Clean serif italic heading like other sections
- Simple "View all" link
- Matches the spacing and style of Projects and Writing sections

**Mini apps page:**
- Serif heading matching the Blog page style
- Clean article list with title, tags as subtle pills, and date
- Description in muted text
- Links styled like your existing underlined links with small icons
- Uses your existing color system and spacing

The design is now consistent with your site rather than copying the reference directly. Tags are subtle background pills, links are inline with icons, and the overall feel matches your blog and projects sections.
